sourceList.title: Foundation for Medieval Genealogy - JUDYTA (before 1154-after 12 Dec 1201).

Foundation for Medieval Genealogy - BERNHARD von Ballenstedt m JUDYTA of Poland, daughter of MIESZKO III "Stary/the Old" Prince of Greater Poland, Prince of Krakow & his first wife Erszébet of Hungary (-after 12 Dec 1201).
Erzählende genealogische Stammtafeln zur europäischen Geschichte Band 1, Teilband 1

zh.lifeSummary: 當Prinzessin Judith Piast於1152年出生在波蘭波茲納n斯吉波茲南時，她的父親Mieszko III the Old Duke of Greater Poland26歲，母親Erzsébet Árpád-házi Magyar hercegnő, lengyel fejedelemné24歲。她於1169年與Bernhard III. Herzog von Sachsen Fürst von Anhalt在波蘭維爾可波爾斯吉波茲南結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1201年12月12日在德國薩辛德累斯頓享年49歲，埋葬在德國薩辛-安哈爾特可德林波格巴倫斯泰特巴倫斯泰特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Prinzessin Judith Piast于1152年出生在波兰波茲納n斯吉波茲南时，她的父亲Mieszko III the Old Duke of Greater Poland26岁，母亲Erzsébet Árpád-házi Magyar hercegnő, lengyel fejedelemné24岁。她于1169年与Bernhard III. Herzog von Sachsen Fürst von Anhalt在波兰維爾可波爾斯吉波茲南结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1201年12月12日在德国薩辛德累斯頓享年49岁，埋葬在德国薩辛-安哈爾特可德林波格巴倫斯泰特巴倫斯泰特。

nameMeanings.name: Piast
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French and Walloon Piat: from the personal name Piat, from the Latin name Piatus ‘expiated’ (from the past participle of the verb pio ‘to appease; to expiate’). The name was borne by Saint Piatus of Tournai (in Wallonia, Belgium), who was martyred under the Roman Emperor Maximian c. 286 AD . Altered ending of the surname reflects the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t. Compare Pyatt .
English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of   Pyatt  .

History: This surname (see 1 above) is listed along with the variant Pyatt in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Pyatt
meanings: English (mainly Staffordshire): nickname either from Middle English piot ‘magpie’ (Old French pïot, a diminutive of Old French pie), commonly used to denote a chattering woman, or alternatively from an unrecorded Middle English pyard, an uncomplimentary term derived from the same Old French word + -(h)ard. Compare Pye .
Variant of   Piatt  , a surname of French and Walloon origin.
History: This surname (see 2 above) is listed along with the variant Piatt in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

nameMeanings.name: Judith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, meaning ‘Jewess’ or ‘woman from Judea’, borne by a Jewish heroine whose story is recorded in the Book of Judith in the Apocrypha. Judith is portrayed as a beautiful widow who delivers her people from the invading Assyrians by gaining the confidence of their commander, Holofernes, and cutting off his head while he is asleep; without their commander, the Assyrians are duly routed. This has been a perennially popular Jewish name. In the English-speaking world it was taken up in the 16th century, having been in occasional use among Gentiles before this: for example, it was borne by a niece of William the Conqueror. It enjoyed great popularity between the 1940s and the 1960s. Today's notable bearers include the American novelist  Judith Krantz  (  b. 1928 ) and the Scottish composer  Judith Weir  (  b. 1954 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Prinzessin Judith Piast was born in 1152, in Poznań, Poznań, Poland, her father, Mieszko III the Old Duke of Greater Poland, was 26 and her mother, Erzsébet Árpád-házi Magyar hercegnő, lengyel fejedelemné, was 24. She married Bernhard III. Herzog von Sachsen Fürst von Anhalt in 1169, in Poznań, Greater Poland, Poland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 12 December 1201, in Dresden, Saxony, Germany, at the age of 49, and was buried in Ballenstedt, Ballenstedt, Quedlinburg, Saxony-Anhalt, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Prinzessin Judith Piast est née en 1152, à Poznań, poznańskie, Pologne, son père, Mieszko III the Old Duke of Greater Poland, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Erzsébet Árpád-házi Magyar hercegnő, lengyel fejedelemné, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Bernhard III. Herzog von Sachsen Fürst von Anhalt en 1169, à Poznań, wielkopolskie, Pologne. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 12 décembre 1201, à Dresden, Sachsen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterrée à Ballenstedt, Ballenstedt, Quedlinburg, Sachsen-Anhalt, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Prinzessin Judith Piast 1152 in Posen, poznańskie, Polen geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Mieszko III the Old Duke of Greater Poland 26 und ihre Mutter Erzsébet Árpád-házi Magyar hercegnő, lengyel fejedelemné 24. Sie hat 1169 in Poznań, Woiwodschaft Großpolen, Polen Bernhard III. Herzog von Sachsen Fürst von Anhalt geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 12. Dezember 1201 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Dresden, Sachsen, Deutschland und wurde in Ballenstedt, Ballenstedt, Quedlinburg, Sachsen-Anhalt, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Prinzessin Judith Piast è nata il nel 1152 nel Poznań, poznańskie, Polonia, sua padre, Mieszko III the Old Duke of Greater Poland, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Erzsébet Árpád-házi Magyar hercegnő, lengyel fejedelemné, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Bernhard III. Herzog von Sachsen Fürst von Anhalt nel 1169, nel Poznań, wielkopolskie, Polonia. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 12 dicembre 1201, nel Dresden, Sachsen, Germania, all'età di 49 anni, e fu sepolta a Ballenstedt, Ballenstedt, Quedlinburg, Sachsen-Anhalt, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Prinzessin Judith Piastが1152年にポーランドポズナnスキポズナnで生まれた時，父Mieszko III the Old Duke of Greater Polandは26歳，母Erzsébet Árpád-házi Magyar hercegnő, lengyel fejedelemnéは24歳であった。1169年にポーランドウィエルコプオルスキポズナnでBernhard III. Herzog von Sachsen Fürst von Anhaltと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1201年12月12日にドイツサチセンドレスデンで49歳で死去し，ドイツサチセン-アンハルトクエドリンブルグボーレンステトボーレンステトにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Prinzessin Judith Piast(이)가 1152년 폴란드 포즈나n스키 포즈나n에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Mieszko III the Old Duke of Greater Poland(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Erzsébet Árpád-házi Magyar hercegnő, lengyel fejedelemné(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1169년 폴란드 위코포스키 포즈나n에서 Bernhard III. Herzog von Sachsen Fürst von Anhalt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1201년 12월 12일 독일 작센 드레즈든에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 작센-아할트 크들린버그 바렌스테드트 바렌스테드트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Prinzessin Judith Piast nasceu em 1152, em Poznań, poznańskie, Polônia, seu pai, Mieszko III the Old Duke of Greater Poland, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Erzsébet Árpád-házi Magyar hercegnő, lengyel fejedelemné, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Bernhard III. Herzog von Sachsen Fürst von Anhalt em 1169, em Poznań, wielkopolskie, Polônia. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 12 de dezembro de 1201, em Dresden, Sachsen, Alemanha, com 49 anos, e foi sepultada em Ballenstedt, Ballenstedt, Quedlinburg, Sachsen-Anhalt, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Prinzessin Judith Piast родилась в 1152 г. в местности познаn, познаnски, Польша, её отцу по имени Mieszko III the Old Duke of Greater Poland было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Erzsébet Árpád-házi Magyar hercegnő, lengyel fejedelemné, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Bernhard III. Herzog von Sachsen Fürst von Anhalt в 1169 г. в местности познаn, виелкополски, Польша. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 12 декабря 1201 г. в местности дресден, сачсен, Германия в возрасте 49 лет и была похоронена в местности балленстедт, балленстедт, куедлинбург, сачсен-анэлт, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Prinzessin Judith Piast nació en 1152, en Poznań, poznańskie, Polonia, su papá, Mieszko III the Old Duke of Greater Poland, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Erzsébet Árpád-házi Magyar hercegnő, lengyel fejedelemné, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Bernhard III. Herzog von Sachsen Fürst von Anhalt en 1169, en Poznań, wielkopolskie, Polonia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 12 de diciembre de 1201, en Dresden, Sachsen, Alemania, a la edad de 49 años, y fue sepultada en Ballenstedt, Ballenstedt, Quedlinburg, Sachsen-Anhalt, Alemania.
